title: Bridge Essay: Prometheus unbound? Oceanic affectivity and the neuropsychodynamics of addiction

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Visionary Cosmologies** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Oceanic affectivity in addiction — neuropsychodynamic model linking altered states to reward dysregulation, reframing oceanic experience as neuroaffective phenomenon

**Prometheus unbound? Oceanic affectivity and the neuropsychodynamics of addiction.**. Frontiers in psychology. (2026). PMID: 41783322. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2026.1699816. Altered states of consciousness range from psychotic disintegration to mystical union, with oceanic affectivity - experiences of ego dissolution, boundlessness, and affective merging - constituting acentral feature. While such states can be induced by psychedelics, they also occur through trauma, meditative practices, or within compulsive addictive cycles.
